Fix school score: support naplan_score field + convert to percentile

Bug: School data uses naplan_score (absolute 300-700) but score calculation
looked for naplan_percentile (0-100), always getting 0 → school_score=0.

Fix: Read both fields, convert naplan_score to percentile if needed.
Applied to: /score endpoint, /rankings endpoint, and AI profile builder.

commit.message:
Update all context topics with cross-protocol coverage

PR #98 added the pendle_morpho topic and updated the tool description,
but the reference topics behind it still spoke Spectra-only. A cold-start
agent calling mv_get_protocol_context(topic="workflow_routing") would get
guidance that didn't mention Pendle tools at all.

Updated 7 topics:
- pt_yt_mechanics: Pendle AMM/YT/SY differences
- position_analysis: pendle_get_portfolio + morpho_get_positions
- looping: Pendle looping, morpho_get_history, morpho_monitor_risk
- networks: full Pendle chain listing with overlap/Pendle-only breakdown
- deposit_path: Pendle entry paths (buy PT, buy YT direct, LP, loop, no auto-roll)
- glossary: SY, logit AMM, vePENDLE, MetaVault, Health Factor definitions
- workflow_routing: wallet analysis queries all 3 protocols, YT arb runs both
  scanners, term structure uses both yield curves, discovery tools lists all 6

Also removed hardcoded Morpho chain lists from CLAUDE.md and context topics —
Morpho markets can exist on any chain where Morpho is deployed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix credit colors, glow animation, purchase confirmation dialog

- Credits bar: green (healthy), amber (low ≤2), red (expiring ≤5 days)

- Purchase confirmation dialog with pack details before buying
- Update Bert's email to bertduff@gmail.com, lookup by email dynamically
- Bert's seed data works whether he signed in via Google or not

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): trigger token refresh on any 401, not just TOKEN_EXPIRED code

When the access token cookie expires the browser stops sending it entirely,
so the protect middleware returns 401 without a code field. The old interceptor
only refreshed when code === "TOKEN_EXPIRED", which was never reached in
practice because the cookie and JWT share the same maxAge. Now we attempt a
silent refresh on any 401 from a non-auth endpoint, preventing the 15-minute
forced logout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor caller identity into MASTER_NAME/MASTER_TITLE/CALLER_NAME/CALLER_TITLE

MASTER_NAME/MASTER_TITLE: 의뢰인 이름과 직함 (예: 오한 / 박사)
CALLER_NAME: AI 비서의 이름 (선택, 예: 인공지능)
CALLER_TITLE: AI 비서의 역할 (예: 비서)
→ "오한 박사님의 인공지능 비서입니다"

commit.message:
chore: remove dead code and fix vercel.json maxDuration

Deleted files (confirmed dead — zero live imports):
- app/api/workers/_deprecated_enrich-product/  explicitly deprecated, old V3 pipeline
- lib/logging.ts                               only imported by deprecated worker above
- app/api/cron/regenerate-all-products/        uses process.env directly (security), superseded
- lib/shopify/source-fusion.ts                 only in commented-out import in ai-enrichment-v3
- code_summary.md                              auto-generated, not code
- scripts/generate-blog-articles.ts            standalone script, cannot run on Vercel serverless
- scripts/generate-launch-articles.ts          same
- scripts/taya-improver.ts                     same (creates git PRs, incompatible with serverless)

Fixed vercel.json:
- maxDuration: 300 → 60 for all cron routes and regenerate-product worker
  This was overriding the route-level export const maxDuration = 60 fixes
  and was causing 405 Method Not Allowed on Vercel Pro (max 60s per function)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(infra): phase 6 — Docker deployment, Aspire orchestration, and security hardening (#137)

* feat(infra): add DbSeeder CLI tool (MGG-206)

Standalone console app that seeds roles and the initial admin user.
References Infrastructure for Identity services and DatabaseSeederService.
Runs after DbMigrator in the Docker entrypoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(infra): add non-root user, CLI tools, and entrypoint to Dockerfile (MGG-52)

Build and include DbMigrator and DbSeeder CLI tools in the Docker image.
Add docker-entrypoint.sh that runs migrations, seeding, then starts API.
Add non-root user for container security. Add OCI image labels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(infra): improve env config and secrets generation (MGG-58)

Enhance .env.example with documented variable descriptions and Identity
password rules. Upgrade generate-secrets.sh to output a complete .env
file including admin password generation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(infra): integrate DbMigrator and DbSeeder into Aspire AppHost (MGG-207)

Wire CLI tools into the Aspire dependency chain:
postgres -> receiptsdb -> db-migrator -> db-seeder -> api -> frontend.
Uses WaitForCompletion() so API only starts after migrations and seeding
finish. AdminSeed config passed via Aspire parameters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(api): remove inline migration and seeding from API startup (MGG-208)

Migrations and seeding now run via DbMigrator and DbSeeder CLI tools,
orchestrated by Aspire (dev) or docker-entrypoint.sh (production).
Update AGENTS.md EF migrations command to use DbMigrator as startup project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(infra): add deployment guide with security checklist (MGG-60, MGG-65)

Step-by-step deployment guide for Raspberry Pi with Docker Compose and
Nginx Proxy Manager. Covers prerequisites, quick start, NPM/SSL config,
operations (update/rollback/backup), troubleshooting, security hardening,
firewall setup, and resource usage targets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(api,infra): add config-driven rate limiting with audit logging (MGG-70)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(infra): increase HEALTHCHECK start-period and document migration-first workflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(infra): use --force on groupadd to handle pre-existing GID in base image

The aspnet:10.0-noble base image already has GID 1654, causing
`groupadd` to fail with exit code 4 in CI Docker builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ci): consolidate PR title check into CI workflow and gate all jobs

Move the Conventional Commits check from a standalone workflow into the
main CI workflow as a gate job. All other jobs now depend on it via
`needs: [pr-title]` — if the PR title is invalid, no CI resources are
wasted running builds, tests, or Docker checks.

On push-to-main events the pr-title job is skipped and downstream jobs
proceed normally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(infra): use built-in .NET base image non-root user instead of creating one

The aspnet:10.0-noble image already includes an `app` user (UID/GID
1654). Creating a custom user with the same IDs fails. Use the
existing user directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add Docker Setup section to README (MGG-288)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(infra): self-contained Docker deployment with auto-generated secrets

Replace .env-based secret management with auto-generated file secrets.
An init container generates secrets to a shared volume on first run,
the entrypoint bridges them to env vars, and gosu handles PUID/PGID
user mapping — copy the compose file, run docker compose up, done.

- Install gosu in Dockerfile, remove USER directive
- Rewrite entrypoint with TZ, PUID/PGID, secrets bridge, gosu exec
- Self-contained docker-compose.yml with init/db/app services
- Add cap_drop: ALL with minimal cap_add on all containers
- Delete .env.example and scripts/generate-secrets.sh
- Update README, deployment docs, and security checklist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Security hardening: geo-IP blocking, auth on webhook debug, remove JWT fallback secret

- Block non-US IPs via Cloudflare cf-ipcountry header (Tor/unknown also blocked)
- Webhook POST exempt from geo-block (Railway infra may be outside US)
- Webhook GET debug endpoint now requires authentication (was fully public)
- Remove dangerous "fallback-change-me-in-production" JWT secret — app now fails
  fast if SESSION_SECRET/RAILWAY_API_TOKEN are missing
- Add webhook secret verification (optional WEBHOOK_SECRET env var)
- Add security headers (CSP, Permissions-Policy) to ALL responses including public paths
- Strip raw headers from webhook debug output to prevent token leakage

commit.message:
Initial commit: AI Call Agent

Twilio + OpenAI 기반 AI 전화 대리 통화 에이전트.
하드코딩된 개인정보를 환경변수(CALLER_NAME, CALLER_TITLE)로 일반화.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set maxDuration = 60 across all API routes for Vercel Pro

Vercel Pro supports max 60s for Serverless Functions.
Routes with maxDuration = 300 or 90 were causing 405 Method Not Allowed
because the route was rejected before execution.

Routes updated (9 files):
- app/api/workers/regenerate-product/route.ts       300 → 60
- app/api/test/pipeline/route.ts                    300 → 60
- app/api/cron/auto-process-products/route.ts       300 → 60
- app/api/cron/blog-researcher/route.ts             300 → 60
- app/api/gateway/execute/route.ts                  300 → 60

- app/api/admin/reset-products/route.ts             300 → 60

Fix disk full: move archive cache to /tmp, restore DB to /data

Root cause: the Kaggle archive dataset (213 WAV files) was stored
on the same persistent disk (/data) as the SQLite database, filling
the 1GB disk completely. Uploads failed with DATABASE_FULL.

Fix: archive audio now downloads to /tmp (ephemeral, re-downloadable)
while SQLite stays on the durable /data persistent disk. Also removes
the /tmp/farts.db emergency fallback — no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: distributed QStash delay for bulk enrichment — prevent Gemini rate-limit storm

Each product worker uses ~5-7 Gemini calls. Without staggering, queuing N products
simultaneously causes N*7 concurrent AI calls at t=0, saturating the 15 RPM limit
and triggering retry storms.

## Changes

### lib/queue/index.ts
- Added `options?: { delaySeconds?: number }` to `queueProductEnrichment`
- Passes `delay: options.delaySeconds` to QStash `publishJSON` when set
- The `delay` field instructs QStash to hold the message and deliver it to the
  worker only after N seconds — the publish call itself is still instant (~50ms)

### app/api/cron/force-enrich-all/route.ts
- Added `index` to the product map callback
- Each product is queued with `delaySeconds: index * 30`
- 50 products → delivered at t=0s, t=30s, ..., t=1470s (24.5 min)
- Stays at or below 15 RPM for the duration of the bulk run

### app/api/cron/process-products-batch/route.ts (rewrite)
- Replaced synchronous worker calls (broken: each worker takes 60-120s, causing
  Vercel 90s timeout on any batch > 1) with QStash queuing
- Added `export const maxDuration = 30` (fast: only does Shopify GraphQL + QStash publish)
- Uses `(startIndex + localIndex) * 30` as delay so batches from successive
  dashboard calls don't collide (e.g. batch 1 products 0-4 at 0/30/60/90/120s,
  batch 2 products 5-9 at 150/180/210/240/270s)
- Added `price` and `images(first:1)` to GraphQL query to populate EnrichmentJob fully
- Response shape is backwards-compatible with BulkSyncPanel in the dashboard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: ImageAgent V4 — 3-step cascade with real og:image extraction

Replaced the broken mock-based image search with a production-ready
3-step cascade that actually finds and validates product images.

## What was broken
- search-client.ts fell back to mock URLs (https://domain.com/mock-result-N)
  when no search API key was configured — Shopify rejected all uploads
- extractImageUrlFromPage() only checked if the URL itself ended in .jpg
  (never true for product pages) or matched a single Toolstop URL pattern
- Gemini was asked to extract image URLs from page titles+snippets without
  seeing any HTML — it hallucinated plausible-looking but invalid URLs

## Fixes (8 total)

1. searchProductImages() now returns [] instead of mock data when no
   provider is configured — avoids fake URLs that cause Shopify failures

2. New searchImagesWithSerpApi() uses engine=google_images which returns
   direct CDN image URLs (not product page URLs)

3. New searchImagesWithGoogle() uses searchType=image, imgType=photo,
   imgSize=large via Google Custom Search API

4. Replaced extractImageUrlFromPage() with fetchOgImageFromPage() — does
   real HTTP fetch of the product page, reads first 50KB, extracts og:image
   then twitter:image meta tags (always the canonical product image)

5. decodeHtmlEntities() — HTML standard encodes & as & in attributes;
   applied to all og:image/twitter:image URLs extracted from HTML

6. HTML entity decoding in searchImagesWithSerpApi() — SerpAPI occasionally
   HTML-encodes URLs in its JSON responses (& → &)

7. maximizeImageUrl() — strips thumbnail resize params (w=200&h=200 etc.)
   when value < 400px to get full-resolution images from CDNs

8. isValidImageUrl() extended — rejects social media tile paths:
   /facebook/, /twitter/, /instagram/, /linkedin/, social-tile, -tile.jpg

## New 3-step cascade (all 3 search functions)
- Step A: searchProductImages() → direct image URLs from Google Images API
- Step B: performWebSearch() + fetchOgImageFromPage() → real HTML + og:image
- Step C: Gemini on snippets (last resort, kept as fallback only)

## Test result (Milwaukee H800E AVR — ID: 10139022491990)
- method: official_site, source: milwaukeetool.com, success: true
- URL: https://www.milwaukeetool.com/--/web-images/sc/e2b66b178df34e4cbda5feae756102db
- has_amp: false (entities decoded), full-res (resize params stripped)

commit.message:
feat: bulk initial sync — cache all text files on guest for instant opens

Host sends all text/source files (up to 1MB each) via drip-feed after
FILE_TREE response. Guest caches in parallel arrays with charCodeAt
comparison (Perry Map unreliable for string keys). File opens check
cache first, falling back to FILE_REQ only for uncached files.

Protocol: BULK_SYNC_START|count → FILE_DATA messages → BULK_SYNC_END
Includes: isTextFile extension whitelist, .app bundle exclusion,
auto-open first .ts file, status bar progress during sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add skills key to plugin.json for marketplace discovery (#23)

## Summary

- Adds `"skills": "./skills/"` to `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` —
required for Claude Code plugin marketplaces to discover and index skill
definitions
- Normalizes `author` from a plain string (`"Databricks"`) to the
standard object format (`{"name": "Databricks"}`)
- Adds `repository` and `keywords` metadata for richer marketplace
listings

## Why

Claude Code plugin marketplaces use the `skills` key in `plugin.json` to
locate skill definitions. Without it, catalog generators skip the plugin
entirely — meaning the two skill sets (`databricks` and
`databricks-apps`) aren't discoverable through any marketplace that
indexes this repo.

The `author` field as a plain string can also cause parsing issues in
marketplaces that expect the standard object format.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `claude plugin marketplace add` still works with the
updated `plugin.json`
- [ ] Confirm marketplace catalog generators can now discover skills in
`./skills/`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ERC-4626 convertToAssets selector — was calling convertToShares

The ERC4626_SELECTORS constant had the wrong function selector:
- OLD: 0xc6e6f592 (convertToShares — returns inverse rate)
- NEW: 0x07a2d13a (convertToAssets — returns actual conversion rate)

This caused mv_check_ibt_health to report inverted rates for all
ERC-4626 IBTs. Healthy appreciating vaults (e.g. sUSDp at 1.0425)
were shown as sub-1.0 (0.959) and flagged as WARNING "potential
value loss" — a false positive on every IBT health check.

Discovered by curling the Avalanche RPC directly and comparing
convertToAssets vs convertToShares outputs against tool results.
